mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 5.5% signals tightening labor markets, which drives corporate profit growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 5.5% show negative or neutral performance (mean < 0.1% with p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in residential construction permits (HOUST) above 1.1 million units signals strong housing demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in high HOUST periods show negative or neutral performance (mean < 0.1% with p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 12-month moving average indicates expanding manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO expansion phases show negative or neutral performance (mean < 0.1% with p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signals robust economic demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show negative correlation with combined GDPC1/RSAFS growth (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank rate hike expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high CPI periods show positive or neutral performance (mean > 0.1% with p < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k indicates weak labor market conditions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in low PAYEMS periods show positive or neutral performance (mean > 0.1% with p < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 6-month moving average signals manufacturing weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO contraction phases show positive or neutral performance (mean > 0.1% with p < 0.1).
